letters.label: Letter One
variants:
My name is Edda Marsh.

I am writing this from a seat that is not mine.

My seat was three carriages behind. I left it quickly. Not in fear. Closer to the way you leave a room when the kettle has been on too long.

I will explain when I have walked further. For now: I have moved. I have begun moving.

Edda Marsh

Edda Marsh.

This seat is warm. Not from me.

My seat is back the way I came. Three carriages, perhaps four. I did not count.

My shawl is still on it. Folded. Folding was the last thing I did not do.

More when I have walked further.

Edda Marsh

letters.label: Letter Two
variants:
I am in a sleeping car. The bed has been made. Not by me.

Last night something was at my old window.

Three heads. Bone the colour of wet ash. The middle larger than the others.

They moved one at a time. The middle would still and the two beside it would turn. All three looked in.

I did not look away. There seemed no reason to. After a while they were gone.

I waited an hour. Then I got up. Then I walked.

Edda Marsh

A sleeping car. The bed is made. Not by me.

Three heads at the old window last night.

Bone-grey. Middle one larger. The two beside it turned when it did not.

They all looked in.

I have looked at worse things in my life. I looked back.

After a while they were gone. I waited. I walked.

Edda Marsh

letters.label: Letter Three
variants:
I walked forward four carriages. I am writing from a reading nook in what may be a library car. Some of the books are real. Some are spine-only and glued to the wall.

It is here too. At my window. Not the one I left. This new one.

The same three heads. The middle one tilted slightly off centre, as if listening.

I walked to the other side of the car. That window was dark. I came back. It was at mine again.

Edda Marsh

Four carriages forward. A library car. Some of the books are real. Some are spines glued to the wall.

It is here too. At this window. Not the one I left.

Same three heads. Middle one tilted, as if listening.

I crossed the car. The other window was dark. I came back. Mine was bright with bone again.

Edda Marsh

letters.label: Letter Four
variants:
I am in a goods car. The crates are nailed shut. The light is the only thing not boxed.

I should tell you about Hollin.

He was my husband. He dug soul sand. He sold most of it. He kept a little for himself.

He dug too deep, in the end. Something came up out of the place he was digging.

Three heads. A middle one larger than the others. A tilt to the middle one. As if listening.

Hollin did not come up.

I have been waiting a long time to see that face again. Now I have.

It is at every window I sit by.

Edda Marsh
